title: Ami Horowitz: Should George Washington University Change Its Name?
body:
Washington is my favorite President. This is so depressing.
The man tried to free his slaves but Virginia was a difficult state to work with. Even in death, it was his will that they'd be free, but the state wouldn't allow it for many since his wife was alive. Could he have fought more for abolition? Yeah. Anybody can always do more. But he was a respected man worldwide.
author: @CarlosRodriguez-dh7mm

title: Ami Horowitz: Should George Washington University Change Its Name?

title: What College Students Wish They Knew Before Attending GW
body: I went to GW (SEAS '13) and here's my two cents - take EASY classes. No employer has ever asked me what classes I took, while some have cared about my GPA. You don't get extra points for acing a hard class versus an easy one. Take the easy ones.

title: WHAT I WISH I KNEW BEFORE ATTENDING GW
body: idk why im watching bc im already a student at GW dkjfgh but on the textbook matter, so true!!!! an advice for all the kids out there, if you really don't want to purchase all the textbooks (especially in hard copy) because they really aren't affordable at all, ask a chinese kid in ur class politely and i GUARANTEE they have a PDF version or at least know another kid with a PDF version. I didn't buy a single textbook this semester except for Japanese (because our teachers write their own), and it honestly helped cut down the costs by a Lot. good luck to everyone applying to colleges!!

title: WHAT I WISH I KNEW BEFORE ATTENDING GW
body: Some tips that helped me roll over a ton of money for the next semester: for breakfast, have cereal because 1 box can last you a bit. For dinner, get something big and then save half for lunch the next day. For holidays, get relatives to get you CVS and Starbucks gift cards because those places can be more expensive than you think!

author: @madeleinevoth6959

title: WHAT I WISH I KNEW BEFORE ATTENDING GW
body:
lol i commented on your other videos too, but i'm a GW alum and the meal expense struggle is REAL at GW. i actually had it worse because i was a freshman in 2014 and that's when we still had J Street. it was technically like a dining hall but they overcharged for bad quality food and we didn't have enough Dining Dollars for it anyway (we had both Colonial Cash and Dining Dollars back in my day...lmao i sound old).
one thing that was always weird for me at GW was the lack of student spirit and campus events. like yeah, individual student organizations had fundraisers and performances sometimes, but there weren't big sports games and campus-wide events that really brought a majority of the student body together. i was fine going out and doing things in the city on weekends with friends, like i do love D.C because there's always something to do, but sometimes i wished we were collectively prouder of our school.
the metro sucks (i'm also from a megacity with an exceptional subway system so i'm just picky), but i do also like the access it gives to other places throughout the DMV. you can go to CUA, American, Howard, GMU, Gallaudet (though i'm not sure anyone ever does go there)...i guess there isn't really a direct metro stop to Georgetown (lol Hoya Suxa) but it's obviously not far and the Georgetown area is fun. there are some interesting places in Maryland and Virginia that are accessible via the metro too. Old Town Alexandria is pretty nice.
author: @jewel7784

title: WHAT I WISH I KNEW BEFORE ATTENDING GW
body:
1st off, not bad; I've watched only a couple of your videos. 
My response:
How expensive: DC area is actually relatively cheap in relative to most cities in the world. However, the amount you spend and what you spend on is up to you.
Meals: There's plenty of cheap options for food in the area, maybe not so much in Foggy Bottom but around Adams Morgan there's some decent cheap options.
Groceries: It's probably not the best that Whole Foods is your closest option for groceries. However, if you're willing to make the extra distance to Trader Joe's, that would be a much cheaper option and plus they have plenty of ready made meals that you can just store in your fridge.
Clubbing/nightlife: I agree, door fees can be expensive. I'm a bit older and the clubbing scene has become less and less of an interest, but if you're just in to finding a place to go out for drinks or craft cocktails, DC is filled with them.
DMV: Totally agree, there is so much to do in the area and fact of how close and accessible it is to get around is definitely a plus. If you're new to the area I personally would recommend just taking a bike and exploring the area and try one of the many diverse food options(Korean, Ethiopian, Thai, Indian, Middle Eastern... the list goes on.).
author: @davidrobles1578

title: WATCH THIS EVERYDAY AND CHANGE YOUR LIFE - Denzel Washington Motivational Speech
body: If you're reading this right now, I just want to say; I'm rooting for you. We’re strangers, but that doesn’t mean I don’t care. I know what it’s like to feel like you're pushing a boulder uphill. I felt that way until I picked up Manifest the Unseen by Luna Rivers I can’t explain it perfectly, but it changed my whole perspective. After checking it out on youtube and reading it things have been flowing—money, peace, confidence. Maybe this is your moment too. Stay open. Wishing you more than you ever imagined. 💫💰

title: How the CIA recruits agents | Minutes With
body: This actually happened to a friend of mine’s son about 8 years ago. While he was in college he told his mom he was approached by ‘someone representing CIA’ who asked if he would like a job and said the CIA liked his ‘world view’. He said he declined the offer. Of course within six months he moved to Germany, became a ‘travel agent’ 😊 bilingual, and goes on secret trips where he can’t be reached for weeks on end. Obv he accepted the assignment.
author: @KM-ul3pf

title: How the CIA recruits agents | Minutes With
body: I’m a CIA officer working undercover in my living room, gathering intel on the couch cushions. No conclusive evidence found yet, need to continue investigating.

title: How the CIA recruits agents | Minutes With
body: What I got from this, more than anything, was the fact that he described his “boss” that way, and the boss had no inclination to clear up those labels about him. It’s telling, in terms of the way the CIA operates. They’ll hire a psychopath/sociopath with possibly violent tendencies with no problem. If anything, they more than likely prefer it.

title: Working In Artificial Intelligence For A Day #shorts #nyc
body: Ohmygod, i discussed my architectural thesis idea with the AI and it was so helpful. I asked it to write a rough synopsis draft for me including the information we exchanged. And it said it'll send it to me via email at the time I say and I said in half an hour. It said ok. I'll see how it is. I'm so surprised. It was so cool to get actual critique instead of random stuff
